title: File management UX: unsaved prompt on close, adjacent tab selection, FileBrowser keyboard access
analysis:
This task addresses 7 file management UX issues across 3 components and 1 store.

**Current state:**
- editorStore.closeFile (line 41) selects remaining[0] instead of the adjacent tab when the active file is closed.
- FileTabs has role='tab' on individual tabs but no role='tablist' on the container div. No tabindex, no keyboard navigation (Left/Right arrows). The close handler (lines 33-36) directly calls store.closeFile with no unsaved-changes check.
- FileBrowser items are plain divs with onClick. No ARIA semantics (no role='listbox'/role='option'), no keyboard accessibility (no tabindex, no key handlers). Only basename shown (no title for full path). Active indicator is background color + font-weight which is subtle.

**Testing patterns:** Tests use vitest + @solidjs/testing-library with fireEvent. Store tests use createRoot((dispose) => {...}) pattern. Components are tested via render() + screen queries + fireEvent. Existing tests for both FileTabs and FileBrowser provide good setup() helpers.

**Approach:** Fix in order: (1) store-level adjacent tab selection, (2) confirmation dialog in FileTabs close handler, (3) tablist semantics + keyboard nav on FileTabs, (4) ARIA + keyboard + title on FileBrowser, (5) enhanced active indicator CSS. Each fix is test-first.

**Key existing code patterns to follow:** ConstraintPanel uses tabindex='0' and onKeyDown for keyboard access. ExportDialog uses role='dialog' with proper ARIA. CSS uses custom properties (--reify-accent, etc.). Tests mock with vi.spyOn().

steps.description: Write failing tests in editorStore.test.ts for adjacent tab selection: (1) close middle tab (B of A,B,C with B active) → selects C (next at same index), (2) close last tab (C of A,B,C with C active) → selects B (previous), (3) close first tab when active (A of A,B with A active) → selects B (next). Update existing test 'closeFile selects first remaining file when active file is closed (3 files)' expectation from 'a.ri' to 'b.ri' since adjacent means the tab at the closed index (b.ri was after c.ri is removed). Also update 'closeFile computes correct fallback inside batch()' similarly.

Fix editorStore.closeFile: before filtering, find closedIndex = state.openFiles.findIndex(f => f.path === path). After filtering to `remaining`, select remaining[closedIndex] ?? remaining[closedIndex - 1] ?? null as the new activeFile. This replaces the current remaining[0] fallback with adjacent-tab selection.

Write failing tests in FileTabs.test.tsx for unsaved-changes prompt on dirty tab close: (1) clicking close on a dirty tab calls window.confirm with message containing the filename, (2) if window.confirm returns false, store.closeFile is NOT called (close cancelled), (3) if window.confirm returns true, store.closeFile IS called, (4) clicking close on a clean tab does NOT call window.confirm and calls closeFile directly. Use vi.spyOn(window, 'confirm') for mocking.

Add confirmation logic in FileTabs close handler: in the onClick of the close button, check if file.path is in props.store.state.dirtyFiles. If dirty, call window.confirm('"<basename>" has unsaved changes. Close anyway?'). Only call props.store.closeFile(file.path) if the user confirms or the file is clean.

Write failing tests in FileTabs.test.tsx for tablist semantics and keyboard navigation: (1) tab bar container has role='tablist', (2) active tab has tabindex='0', inactive tabs have tabindex='-1' (roving tabindex), (3) pressing ArrowRight on the active tab activates the next tab, (4) pressing ArrowLeft activates the previous tab, (5) ArrowRight on last tab wraps to first, (6) ArrowLeft on first wraps to last, (7) each tab has a title attribute containing the full file path for disambiguation.

Update FileTabs component: (1) add role='tablist' on the container div, (2) add tabindex={isActive() ? 0 : -1} on each tab div, (3) add title={file.path} on each tab div, (4) add onKeyDown handler on each tab: ArrowRight → setActiveFile to next file (with wrapping), ArrowLeft → setActiveFile to previous (with wrapping), and focus the newly active tab element via ref or querySelector.

Write failing tests in FileBrowser.test.tsx for keyboard accessibility and ARIA semantics: (1) container has role='listbox', (2) container has aria-label='File browser', (3) each file item has role='option', (4) active item has aria-selected='true', non-active has aria-selected='false', (5) each item has title attribute with full file path, (6) active item has tabindex='0', others have tabindex='-1', (7) pressing ArrowDown on focused item moves focus/selection to next item, (8) pressing ArrowUp moves to previous, (9) pressing Enter on focused item calls onFileClick with that file's path.

Update FileBrowser component: (1) add role='listbox' and aria-label='File browser' on container div, (2) change file items to use role='option' with aria-selected={isActive ? 'true' : 'false'}, (3) add title={file.path} on each item, (4) add tabindex={isActive ? 0 : -1} (roving tabindex), (5) add onKeyDown handler: ArrowDown → focus next item and call onFileClick, ArrowUp → focus previous, Enter/Space → call onFileClick. Use refs or DOM queries to manage focus.

Write failing test in FileBrowser.test.tsx: active file item has a visually distinct left-border indicator — verify the active item has a CSS class or computed style indicating the enhanced border (test via checking the data-active attribute styling or a dedicated CSS class).

Update FileBrowser.module.css: enhance .item[data-active='true'] with border-left: 3px solid var(--reify-accent, #89b4fa) and adjust padding-left to compensate (9px instead of 12px). Add border-left: 3px solid transparent to .item base for consistent alignment. This makes the active file unmistakably visible.

design_decisions.decision: Confirmation uses window.confirm() in the FileTabs component rather than a custom dialog or store-level logic
design_decisions.rationale: window.confirm is synchronous and simple. The store stays pure/testable without side effects. A custom modal would be over-engineering for this use case. The confirm call lives in the component's click handler, making it easy to mock in tests via vi.spyOn(window, 'confirm').

design_decisions.decision: Adjacent tab selection uses closedIndex position: prefer next tab (same index in remaining), fall back to previous
design_decisions.rationale: This matches VS Code and most IDE behavior. When you close a middle tab, the tab to its right slides into position. When you close the rightmost tab, the previous tab is selected. remaining[closedIndex] ?? remaining[closedIndex-1] implements this correctly because after filtering, the element at closedIndex in remaining is what was originally at closedIndex+1.

design_decisions.decision: Use roving tabindex pattern for both FileTabs and FileBrowser keyboard navigation
design_decisions.rationale: Roving tabindex (active item gets tabindex=0, others tabindex=-1) is the WAI-ARIA recommended pattern for composite widgets like tablists and listboxes. It allows a single Tab press to enter/exit the widget, and arrow keys to move within it.

design_decisions.decision: FileBrowser uses role='listbox' with role='option' children rather than converting to <button> elements
design_decisions.rationale: role='listbox' with role='option' children is the correct ARIA pattern for a single-select file list. It communicates the selection semantics properly to screen readers. Items keep their div element but gain proper roles.

design_decisions.decision: Full path shown via title attribute on tabs and file items, not by changing displayed text
design_decisions.rationale: A title attribute provides a tooltip with the full path on hover without cluttering the compact UI. This matches VS Code's approach for short tab labels with full-path tooltips.

design_decisions.decision: Active file indicator enhancement uses left border (3px solid accent) with transparent border on inactive items for alignment
design_decisions.rationale: A colored left border provides a strong visual signal without causing layout shift. Adding transparent borders on all items prevents the layout jumping when an item becomes active. The existing style already has background + font-weight changes; the left border makes it unmistakable.

reuse.what: createRoot() + dispose() pattern for store unit tests
reuse.how: All new store tests wrap in createRoot((dispose) => { ... dispose(); }) — same pattern as all existing editorStore tests.

reuse.what: setup() helper function with files and opts.dirty
reuse.how: Reuse the existing setup(files, { dirty }) helper for creating stores with dirty files for the confirmation dialog tests.
Use @solidjs/testing-library render with arrow function, screen queries by testid/role/text, fireEvent for user interactions.
Use existing --reify-accent, --reify-surface, --reify-text CSS custom properties for the left border color and any new styles.

reuse.what: onKeyDown handler pattern from ConstraintPanel and Splitter
reuse.how: Follow the same inline onKeyDown with switch/if on e.key pattern, calling e.preventDefault() for handled keys.

reuse.what: vi.spyOn pattern for mocking store methods in component tests
reuse.how: Use vi.spyOn(store, 'closeFile') and vi.spyOn(store, 'setActiveFile') as already done; extend with vi.spyOn(window, 'confirm') for the confirmation dialog tests.
Follow existing patterns: 'file-tab', 'file-browser', 'close-tab', 'file-item-{path}' for testable element identifiers.
